name: kargha
description: Stack-agnostic, ad-hoc orchestration framework. kargha synthesizes a binder of work items (kargha-plan) and delivers it in parallel waves onto a per-binder integration branch (kargha-deliver), building each item in an isolated git worktree (kargha-build) and verifying it against its own acceptance check — behavioral (kargha-verify) or visual (kargha-validate). Works across stacks (frontend, backend, CLI, data, IaC, …) while keeping deep frontend support: component/icon/token mapping, DTCG conformance, and a design-validation loop.

interface.shortDescription: Synthesize a binder and deliver it in parallel waves, gated by acceptance checks.
interface.longDescription: kargha packages five stack-agnostic orchestration skills and two read-only gate agents. kargha-plan synthesizes a validated binder of work items; kargha-deliver builds them in parallel waves onto a per-binder integration branch; kargha-build implements one item in an isolated worktree; kargha-verify (behavioral) and kargha-validate (visual) are the acceptance gates. Stack-agnostic across frontend, backend, CLI, data, and IaC, while keeping deep frontend support (component/token mapping, DTCG, design validation).
defaultPrompt: Synthesize a kargha binder for this work.
Deliver this binder in parallel waves.
Validate this running view against the design.
